component added and setup\\\")\\n00307| \\n00308| \\n00309| ## Setup warm ceiling lights in the centers of large rooms (Issue #1206).\\n00310| ## Adds PointLight2D nodes with warm yellow-orange color to make the rooms\\n00311| ## look cozy and aesthetically pleasing.\\n00312| ##\\n00313| ## ## Light placement rules\\n00314| ## 1. Default position: geometric center of the room (average of its bounding box).\\n00315| ## 2. If a large obstacle (table, server rack, wall junction, etc.) sits at the\\n00316| ##    geometric center, shift the light to the nearest open area — typically\\n00317| ##    offset toward the side that has the most free floor space.\\n00318| ## 3. If a wall junction or shadow-casting surface is close (< 30 px) to the\\n00319| ##    light source, move the light inward until it is fully inside the open\\n00320| ##    floor area so shadows don't block the cone.\\n00321| ## 4. Prefer the upper half of a room when the lower half is crowded or when\\n00322| ##    the room label (\\\"OFFICE 2\\\", etc.) already anchors the top edge visually.\\n00323| ##\\n00324| ## Room centers (derived from RoomLabel bounds in the scene):\\n00325| ## - Conference Room: ~(1918, 340)  — geometric center, no obstacles\\n00326| ## - Break Room:      ~(1918, 994)  — geometric center, no obstacles\\n00327| ## - Server Room:     ~(2200, 1638) — shifted right, away from right-wall junction\\n00328| ## - Main Hall:       ~(1200, 1724) — geometric center, no obstacles\\n00329| ## - Office 1:        ~(290, 384)   — geometric center, no obstacles\\n00330| ## - Office 2:        ~(718, 780)   — shifted up from center (856) to upper half\\n00331| func _setup_room_warm_lights() -> void:\\n00332| \\tvar environment := get_node_or_null(\\\"Environment\\\")\\n00333| \\tif environment == null:\\n00334| \\t\\treturn\\n00335| \\n00336| \\t# Container node for all room lights\\n00337| \\tvar room_lights_node := Node2D.new()\\n00338| \\troom_lights_node.name = \\\"RoomLights\\\"\\n00339| \\tenvironment.add_child(room_lights_node)\\n00340| \\n00341| \\t# Large rooms get prominent warm lights; smaller rooms get subtler ones.\\n00342| \\t# Format: [position, energy, texture_scale, label]\\n00343| \\tvar room_configs: Array = [\\n00344| \\t\\t# Large rooms — bigger lights\\n00345| \\t\\t[Vector2(1918, 340),  0.9, 5.0, \\\"ConferenceRoom\\\"],\\n00346| \\t\\t[Vector2(1918, 994),  0.9, 5.0, \\\"BreakRoom\\\"],\\n00347| \\t\\t[Vector2(2200, 1638), 0.9, 5.0, \\\"ServerRoom\\\"],\\n00348| \\t\\t[Vector2(1200, 1724), 0.85, 4.5, \\\"MainHall\\\"],\\n00349| \\t\\t# Smaller rooms — softer lights\\n00350| \\t\\t[Vector2(290, 384),   0.7, 3.5, \\\"Office1\\\"],\\n00351| \\t\\t# Office 2: shifted to upper half (y=780 instead of centre y=856)\\n00352| \\t\\t# so the glow covers the label zone and the lower-half corridor approach.\\n00353| \\t\\t[Vector2(718, 780),   0.7, 3.5, \\\"Office2\\\"],\\n00354| \\t]\\n00355| \\n00356| \\tfor cfg in room_configs:\\n00357| \\t\\t_create_room_warm_light(room_lights_node, cfg[0], cfg[1], cfg[2], cfg[3])\\n00358| \\n00359| \\tprint(\\\"[BuildingLevel] Warm ceiling lights placed in all rooms (Issue #1206)\\\")\\n00360| \\n00361| \\n00362| ## Create a single warm ceiling light at the given room-center position.\\n00363| ## Uses a soft radial gradient that fades smoothly to black, producing a natural\\n00364| ## \\\"overhead lamp\\\" feel with no hard visible edge.\\n00365| ## @param parent: Container node.\\n00366| ## @param pos: World-space position (room center).\\n00367| ## @param energy: Light brightness (0–1 range, typical 0.7–0.9).\\n00368| ## @param scale: Texture scale controlling the light radius.\\n00369| ## @param room_name: Name suffix for the node (debug convenience).\\n00370| func _create_room_warm_light(parent: Node2D, pos: Vector2, energy: float, scale: float, room_name: String) -> void:\\n00371| \\tvar light_node := Node2D.new()\\n00372| \\tlight_node.name = \\\"WarmLight_%s\\\" % room_name\\n00373| \\tlight_node.position = pos\\n00374| \\tparent.add_child(light_node)\\n00375| \\n00376| \\t# Small visual indicator — a dim warm-colored circle representing the lamp fixture.\\n00377| \\t# Uses Sprite2D (not Control/ColorRect) so it does NOT intercept mouse events and\\n00378| \\t# cannot break pause-menu or UI clicks.\\n00379| \\tvar fixture := Sprite2D.new()\\n00380| \\tfixture.name = \\\"Fixture\\\"\\n00381| \\tfixture.texture = _create_lamp_fixture_texture()\\n00382| \\tfixture.modulate = Color(1.0, 0.85, 0.5, 0.5)  # Pale warm amber, semi-transparent\\n00383| \\tlight_node.add_child(fixture)\\n00384| \\n00385| \\t# The actual PointLight2D\\n00386| \\tvar light := PointLight2D.new()\\n00387| \\tlight.name = \\\"PointLight\\\"\\n00388| \\tlight.color = Color(1.0, 0.75, 0.3, 1.0)   # Warm amber-orange\\n00389| \\tlight.energy = energy\\n00390| \\tlight.shadow_enabled = true\\n00391| \\tlight.shadow_filter = PointLight2D.SHADOW_FILTER_PCF5\\n00392| \\tlight.shadow_filter_smooth = 4.0  # Soft shadow edges\\n00393| \\tlight.texture = _create_warm_light_texture()\\n00394| \\tlight.texture_scale = scale\\n00395| \\tlight_node.add_child(light)\\n00396| \\n00397| \\n00398| ## Create a soft radial gradient texture for the warm room lights.\\n00399| ## Uses a smooth natural falloff (bright core → gentle taper → complete black).\\n00400| ## No abrupt cutoff — the light fades organically like a real overhead lamp.\\n00401| func _create_warm_light_texture() -> ImageTexture:\\n00402| \\tvar size := 512\\n00403| \\tvar center := Vector2(size * 0.5, size * 0.5)\\n00404| \\tvar outer_r := size * 0.5  # 256 px\\n00405| \\n00406| \\tvar image := Image.create(size, size, false, Image.FORMAT_RGBA8)\\n00407| \\n00408| \\tfor y in range(size):\\n00409| \\t\\tfor x in range(size):\\n00410| \\t\\t\\tvar dist := Vector2(x, y).distance_to(center)\\n00411| \\t\\t\\tvar t := clampf(dist / outer_r, 0.0, 1.0)  # 0 = center, 1 = edge\\n00412| \\t\\t\\t# Smooth inverse-square-ish falloff using a cosine curve:\\n00413| \\t\\t\\t# bright centre → smooth mid-field → natural fade at rim\\n00414| \\t\\t\\tvar brightness := pow(1.0 - t, 2.2)\\n00415| \\t\\t\\timage.set_pixel(x, y, Color(brightness, brightness, brightness, 1.0))\\n00416| \\n00417| \\treturn ImageTexture.create_from_image(image)\\n00418| \\n00419| \\n00420| ## Create a small circular texture for the lamp fixture visual indicator.\\n00421| ## Returns a soft-edged disc so the fixture looks like a round ceiling lamp,\\n00422| ## not a square. Drawn with per-pixel math so the disc has smooth anti-aliased edges.\\n00423| func _create_lamp_fixture_texture() -> ImageTexture:\\n00424| \\tvar size := 32\\n00425| \\tvar center := Vector2(size * 0.5, size * 0.5)\\n00426| \\tvar outer_r := size * 0.5  # full disc radius\\n00427| \\n00428| \\tvar image := Image.create(size, size, false, Image.FORMAT_RGBA8)\\n00429| \\n00430| \\tfor y in range(size):\\n00431| \\t\\tfor x in range(size):\\n00432| \\t\\t\\tvar dist := Vector2(x, y).distance_to(center)\\n00433| \\t\\t\\tif dist >= outer_r:\\n00434| \\t\\t\\t\\timage.set_pixel(x, y, Color(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0))\\n00435| \\t\\t\\telse:\\n00436| \\t\\t\\t\\t# Full brightness in the core, soft fade toward the rim\\n00437| \\t\\t\\t\\tvar t := clampf(dist / outer_r, 0.0, 1.0)\\n00438| \\t\\t\\t\\tvar alpha := pow(1.0 - t, 1.5)\\n00439| \\t\\t\\t\\timage.set_pixel(x, y, Color(1.0, 1.0, 1.0, alpha))\\n00440| \\n00441| \\treturn ImageTexture.create_from_image(image)\\n00442| \\n00443| \\n00444| ## Setup window lights in corridors and rooms without enemies (Issue #593).\\n00445| ## Places dim blue PointLight2D nodes along exterior walls to simulate moonlight\\n00446| ## coming through windows. Only in areas without enemies so dark rooms with enemies\\n00447| ## remain tense and challenging.\\n00448| ## Window visuals (small blue ColorRect) are placed on the wall surface.\\n00449| ##\\n00450| ## Lighting architecture:\\n00451| ## - Each window has ONE primary PointLight2D (low energy, shadows on) for the\\n00452| ##   visible moonlight patch near the window.\\n00453| ## - ONE DirectionalLight2D provides scene-wide ambient moonlight glow with\\n00454| ##   NO visible edges. DirectionalLight2D illuminates the entire scene uniformly\\n00455| ##   (unlike PointLight2D which has a finite circular boundary).\\n00456| func _setup_window_lights() -> void:\\n00457| \\tvar environment := get_node_or_null(\\\"Environment\\\")\\n00458| \\tif environment == null:\\n00459| \\t\\treturn\\n00460| \\n00461| \\t# Create a container node for all window lights\\n00462| \\tvar windows_node := Node2D.new()\\n00463| \\twindows_node.name = \\\"WindowLights\\\"\\n00464| \\tenvironment.add_child(windows_node)\\n00465| \\n00466| \\t# Window light positions: [position, wall_side]\\n00467| \\t# wall_side: \\\"top\\\", \\\"bottom\\\", \\\"left\\\", \\\"right\\\" determines window visual orientation\\n00468| \\t# Placed ONLY in corridors/rooms WITHOUT enemies:\\n00469| \\t# - Central corridor (x=512-1376, y=700-1012)\\n00470| \\t# - Left lobby area (x=64-900, y=1000-1400)\\n00471| \\t# - Storage room (x=80-500, y=1612-2048)\\n00472| \\n00473| \\t# Left wall windows (x=64) - lobby and storage areas (no enemies)\\n00474| \\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(64, 1100), \\\"left\\\")\\n00475| \\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(64, 1250), \\\"left\\\")\\n00476| \\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(64, 1750), \\\"left\\\")\\n00477| \\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(64, 1900), \\\"left\\\")\\n00478| \\n00479| \\t# Top wall windows (y=64) - above corridor area (no enemies in corridor)\\n00480| \\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(700, 64), \\\"top\\\")\\n00481| \\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(900, 64), \\\"top\\\")\\n00482| \\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(1100, 64), \\\"top\\\")\\n00483| \\n00484| \\t# Bottom wall windows (y=2064) - below storage and lobby (no enemies)\\n00485| \\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(200, 2064), \\\"bottom\\\")\\n00486| \\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(400, 2064), \\\"bottom\\\")\\n00487| \\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(700, 2064), \\\"bottom\\\")\\n00488| \\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(1100, 2064), \\\"bottom\\\")\\n00489| \\n00490| \\t# Scene-wide ambient moonlight using DirectionalLight2D.\\n00491| \\t# DirectionalLight2D illuminates the entire scene uniformly with NO visible\\n00492| \\t# edges (unlike PointLight2D which has a finite circular boundary).\\n00493| \\t# Energy is very low (0.06) so it provides visible wall outlines\\n00494| \\t# without washing out the scene or hiding weapon muzzle flashes.\\n00495| \\t_create_ambient_moonlight(windows_node)\\n00496| \\n00497| \\tprint(\\\"[BuildingLevel] Window lights placed in corridors without enemies (Issue #593)\\\")\\n00498| \\n00499| \\n00500| ## Create a single window light source at the given position on a wall.\\n00501| ## Creates ONE PointLight2D with shadows enabled — the visible moonlight patch\\n00502| ## near the window. Shadows from interior walls give the light a natural shape\\n00503| ## that respects the building's geometry.\\n00504| ## The scene-wide ambient glow is handled separately by _create_ambient_moonlight().\\n00505| ##\\n00506| ## Light gradient uses an early-fadeout design: the radial gradient reaches\\n00507| ## absolute zero at 55% of the radius, leaving 45% of the texture as pure black\\n00508| ## buffer. This ensures NO visible edges even against the near-black CanvasModulate.\\n00509| ## Combined with large texture_scale (6.0) and low energy (0.12), the light\\n00510| ## dissipates naturally with no perceptible boundary.\\n00511| ## @param parent: Parent node to add the window to.\\n00512| ## @param pos: Position of the window on the wall.\\n00513| ## @param wall_side: Which wall the window is on (\\\"top\\\", \\\"bottom\\\", \\\"left\\\", \\\"right\\\").\\n00514| func _create_window_light(parent: Node2D, pos: Vector2, wall_side: String) -> void:\\n00515| \\tvar window_node := Node2D.new()\\n00516| \\twindow_node.name = \\\"Window_%s_%d_%d\\\" % [wall_side, int(pos.x), int(pos.y)]\\n00517| \\twindow_node.position = pos\\n00518| \\tparent.add_child(window_node)\\n00519| \\n00520| \\t# Create window visual (small blue rectangle on the wall)\\n00521| \\tvar window_rect := ColorRect.new()\\n00522| \\twindow_rect.color = Color(0.3, 0.4, 0.7, 0.6)  # Semi-transparent blue\\n00523| \\t# Size and offset depend on wall orientation\\n00524| \\tmatch wall_side:\\n00525| \\t\\t\\\"left\\\":\\n00526| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_left = -4.0\\n00527| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_top = -20.0\\n00528| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_right = 4.0\\n00529| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_bottom = 20.0\\n00530| \\t\\t\\\"right\\\":\\n00531| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_left = -4.0\\n00532| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_top = -20.0\\n00533| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_right = 4.0\\n00534| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_bottom = 20.0\\n00535| \\t\\t\\\"top\\\":\\n00536| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_left = -20.0\\n00537| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_top = -4.0\\n00538| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_right = 20.0\\n00539| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_bottom = 4.0\\n00540| \\t\\t\\\"bottom\\\":\\n00541| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_left = -20.0\\n00542| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_top = -4.0\\n00543| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_right = 20.0\\n00544| \\t\\t\\twindow_rect.offset_bottom = 4.0\\n00545| \\twindow_node.add_child(window_rect)\\n00546| \\n00547| \\t# --- Primary moonlight (visible patch near the window) ---\\n00548| \\tvar light := PointLight2D.new()\\n00549| \\tlight.name = \\\"MoonLight\\\"\\n00550| \\tlight.color = Color(0.4, 0.5, 0.9, 1.0)  # Cool blue moonlight\\n00551| \\tlight.energy = 0.12  # Low — slightly brighter (Issue #642), large texture_scale compensates for coverage\\n00552| \\t# Shadows enabled so interior walls cast natural shadows from the moonlight\\n00553| \\tlight.shadow_enabled = true\\n00554| \\tlight.shadow_filter = PointLight2D.SHADOW_FILTER_PCF5\\n00555| \\tlight.shadow_filter_smooth = 4.0  # Higher smoothing for softer shadow edges\\n00556| \\tlight.shadow_color = Color(0, 0, 0, 0.7)\\n00557| \\tlight.texture = _create_window_light_texture()\\n00558| \\tlight.texture_scale = 6.0  # Large scale so gradient fades out well before the edge\\n00559| \\t# Offset the light inward from the wall so it illuminates the interior\\n00560| \\tmatch wall_side:\\n00561| \\t\\t\\\"left\\\":\\n00562| \\t\\t\\tlight.position = Vector2(60, 0)\\n00563| \\t\\t\\\"right\\\":\\n00564| \\t\\t\\tlight.position = Vector2(-60, 0)\\n00565| \\t\\t\\\"top\\\":\\n00566| \\t\\t\\tlight.position = Vector2(0, 60)\\n00567| \\t\\t\\\"bottom\\\":\\n00568| \\t\\t\\tlight.position = Vector2(0, -60)\\n00569| \\twindow_node.add_child(light)\\n00570| \\n00571| \\n00572| ## Create a radial gradient texture for the primary window moonlight.\\n00573| ## Uses an early-fadeout design where the gradient reaches absolute zero at 55%\\n00574| ## of the radius, leaving 45% of the texture as pure black buffer zone.\\n00575| ## This eliminates visible edges at the PointLight2D quad boundary because the\\n00576| ## light contribution is already zero well before the texture boundary.\\n00577| ## Against the near-black CanvasModulate (0.02, 0.02, 0.04), even subpixel\\n00578| ## differences at the boundary could be visible, so the large buffer is critical.\\n00579| func _create_window_light_texture() -> GradientTexture2D:\\n00580| \\tvar gradient := Gradient.new()\\n00581| \\t# Bright center core (0-10% radius)\\n00582| \\tgradient.set_color(0, Color(1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0))\\n00583| \\t# Smooth falloff begins early\\n00584| \\tgradient.add_point(0.1, Color(0.7, 0.7, 0.7, 1.0))\\n00585| \\tgradient.add_point(0.2, Color(0.45, 0.45, 0.45, 1.0))\\n00586| \\tgradient.add_point(0.3, Color(0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 1.0))\\n00587| \\tgradient.add_point(0.4, Color(0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 1.0))\\n00588| \\t# Fade to absolute zero by 55% — remaining 45% is pure black buffer\\n00589| \\tgradient.add_point(0.5, Color(0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 1.0))\\n00590| \\tgradient.add_point(0.55, Color(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0))\\n00591| \\tgradient.set_color(1, Color(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0))\\n00592| \\n00593| \\tvar texture := GradientTexture2D.new()\\n00594| \\ttexture.gradient = gradient\\n00595| \\ttexture.width = 512\\n00596| \\ttexture.height = 512\\n00597| \\ttexture.fill = GradientTexture2D.FILL_RADIAL\\n00598| \\ttexture.fill_from = Vector2(0.5, 0.5)\\n00599| \\ttexture.fill_to = Vector2(0.5, 0.0)\\n00600| \\treturn texture\\n00601| \\n00602| \\n00603| ## Create a scene-wide ambient moonlight using DirectionalLight2D.\\n00604| ## DirectionalLight2D illuminates the entire scene uniformly — unlike PointLight2D,\\n00605| ## it has no position, no radius, and NO visible edges. This is the correct node\\n00606| ## for simulating moonlight (parallel rays from a distant source).\\n00607| ## Shadows are disabled so the ambient glow passes through all walls uniformly.\\n00608| ## @param parent: Parent node to add the ambient light to.\\n00609| func _create_ambient_moonlight(parent: Node2D) -> void:\\n00610| \\tvar ambient := DirectionalLight2D.new()\\n00611| \\tambient.name = \\\"AmbientMoonlight\\\"\\n00612| \\tambient.color = Color(0.35, 0.45, 0.85, 1.0)  # Subtle blue moonlight tint\\n00613| \\tambient.energy = 0.06  # Faint — slightly brighter (Issue #642) for better wall outline visibility\\n00614| \\tambient.shadow_enabled = false  # Must pass through all walls for uniform glow\\n00615| \\tparent.add_child(ambient)\\n00616| \\n00617| \\n00618| func _process(_delta: float) -> void:\\n00619| \\t# Update enemy positions for aggressiveness tracking\\n00620| \\tvar score_manager: Node = get_node_or_null(\\\"/root/ScoreManager\\\")\\n00621| \\tif score_manager and score_manager.has_method(\\\"update_enemy_positions\\\"):\\n00622| \\t\\tscore_manager.update_enemy_positions(_enemies)\\n00623| \\t# Issue #959: Re-check level completion when a retaliating pacifist finishes retaliation.\\n00624| \\tif _current_enemy_count <= 0 and not _level_cleared and not _has_retaliating_pacifists():\\n00625| \\t\\tprint(\\\"All enemies eliminated or pacified! [2026-04-15T16:55:01.537Z] [INFO]  int) -> Color:\\n00662| \\tif combo >= 10:\\n00663| \\t\\treturn Color(1.0, 0.0, 1.0, 1.0)   # Magenta - extreme combo\\n00664| \\telif combo >= 7:\\n00665| reset.\n\tcall_deferred(\"_reset_and_apply_all_unlocks\")\n\t_log(\"UnlockManager ready\")\n\n\n## Called when ProgressManager emits progress_updated after a level is completed.\n## Checks if any unlock conditions are now satisfied (items turn gold in armory).\n## Note: items are NOT auto-unlocked here — the player must hold LMB on the gold\n## slot in the armory to actually unlock them.\nfunc _on_progress_updated(level_path: String, difficulty_name: String) -> void:\n\t# Notify that conditions may have changed so armory can refresh gold highlights.\n\t# Check single-level conditions (any key whose scene path matches this level).\n\tfor condition_key in UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tif _extract_scene_path(condition_key) == level_path and is_condition_key_met(condition_key):\n\t\t\titems_unlocked_by_condition.emit(level_path)\n\t\t\t_log(\"Condition met for level: %s — items now available to unlock in armory\" % level_path)\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t# Check multi-level conditions.\n\tfor multi_condition in MULTI_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tfor level_entry in multi_condition.get(\"levels\", []):\n\t\t\tif level_entry.get(\"path\", \"\") == level_path:\n\t\t\t\tif is_multi_condition_met(multi_condition):\n\t\t\t\t\titems_unlocked_by_condition.emit(level_path)\n\t\t\t\t\t_log(\"Multi-condition met involving level: %s\" % level_path)\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t# Check all-difficulties conditions.\n\tif is_all_difficulties_condition_met():\n\t\titems_unlocked_by_condition.emit(level_path)\n\t\t_log(\"All-difficulties condition met — Experimental Sample now available to unlock in armory\")\n\n\n## Called when GameManager emits kills_without_laser_sight_updated.\n## Checks if any kill-based unlock conditions are now satisfied (items turn gold in armory).\nfunc _on_kills_without_laser_sight_updated(_new_count: int) -> void:\n\tfor kill_condition in KILL_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tif is_kill_condition_met(kill_condition):\n\t\t\titems_unlocked_by_kill_condition.emit()\n\t\t\t_log(\"Kill condition met — items now available to unlock in armory\")\n\t\t\tbreak\n\n\n## Called when _on_no_damage_levels_completed_updated(_new_count: int) -> void:\n\tfor kill_condition in KILL_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tif kill_condition.get(\"stat\", \"\") == \"no_damage_levels_completed\" and _on_levels_completed_rank_a_or_higher_updated(_new_count: int) -> void:\n\tfor kill_condition in KILL_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tif kill_condition.get(\"stat\", \"\") == Called when GameManager emits levels_completed_with_silenced_pistol_updated.\n## Checks if the Auto Reload silenced-pistol level condition is now satisfied.\n## Issue #1624.\nfunc _on_levels_completed_with_silenced_pistol_updated(_new_count: int) -> void:\n\tfor kill_condition in KILL_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tif kill_condition.get(\"stat\", \"\") == available to unlock in armory\")\n\t\t\tbreak\n\n\n## Get the best rank for a level across all difficulties.\n## @param level_path: The scene file path of the level.\n## @return: The best rank string, or \"\" if never completed.\nfunc _get_best_rank_any_difficulty(level_path: String) -> String:\n\tvar progress_manager: Node = get_node_or_null(\"/root/ProgressManager\")\n\tif not progress_manager:\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\n\tvar best_rank: String = \"\"\n\tfor difficulty_name in _get_all_difficulty_names():\n\t\tvar rank: String = progress_manager.get_best_rank(level_path, difficulty_name)\n\t\tif not rank.is_empty() and _is_rank_better(rank, best_rank):\n\t\t\tbest_rank = rank\n\n\treturn best_rank\n\n\n## Check if a rank meets or exceeds the minimum rank requirement.\n## @param rank: The rank to check.\n## @param min_rank: The minimum rank required.\n## @return: true if rank is >= min_rank.\nfunc _is_rank_sufficient(rank: String, min_rank: String) -> bool:\n\tif min_rank == \"F\":\n\t\t# F or higher means any completed run counts\n\t\treturn not rank.is_empty()\n\treturn not _is_rank_better(min_rank, rank)  # min_rank is not better than rank => rank >= min_rank\n\n\n## Compare two ranks. Returns true if new_rank is strictly better than old_rank.\nfunc _is_rank_better(new_rank: String, old_rank: String) -> bool:\n\tif old_rank.is_empty():\n\t\treturn true\n\tvar new_index: int = RANK_ORDER.find(new_rank)\n\tvar old_index: int = RANK_ORDER.find(old_rank)\n\tif new_index == -1:\n\t\treturn false\n\tif old_index == -1:\n\t\treturn true\n\treturn new_index > old_index\n\n\n## Extract the actual scene path from a condition key.\n## Keys may have a rank suffix like \"res://scenes/levels/BuildingLevel.tscn:S\".\n## Returns the scene path part only.\nfunc _extract_scene_path(condition_key: String) -> String:\n\t# Strip \":RANK\" suffix if present (the suffix won't start with \"res://\")\n\tvar last_colon: int = condition_key.rfind(\":\")\n\tif last_colon > 0 and not condition_key.substr(0, last_colon).ends_with(\"//\"):\n\t\t# The suffix is a rank like \":S\", \":D\", etc. (short, no slashes)\n\t\tvar suffix: String = condition_key.substr(last_colon + 1)\n\t\tif suffix in RANK_ORDER:\n\t\t\treturn condition_key.substr(0, last_colon)\n\treturn condition_key\n\n\n## Check if a specific condition key's unlock condition is currently met (for UI highlighting).\n## @param condition_key: The key in UNLOCK_CONDITIONS (may have rank suffix).\n## @return: true if the condition has been met (item can be unlocked).\nfunc is_condition_key_met(condition_key: String) -> bool:\n\tif condition_key not in UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\treturn false\n\tvar condition: Dictionary = UNLOCK_CONDITIONS[condition_key]\n\tvar min_rank: String = condition.get(\"min_rank\", \"D\")\n\tvar scene_path: String = _extract_scene_path(condition_key)\n\tvar best_rank: String = _get_best_rank_any_difficulty(scene_path)\n\treturn _is_rank_sufficient(best_rank, min_rank)\n\n\n## Check if a specific level's unlock condition is currently met (for UI highlighting).\n## Checks all condition keys that match this level path.\n## @param level_path: The scene file path of the level.\n## @return: true if any condition for this level has been met.\nfunc is_level_condition_met(level_path: String) -> bool:\n\tfor condition_key in UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tif _extract_scene_path(condition_key) == level_path:\n\t\t\tif is_condition_key_met(condition_key):\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\treturn false\n\n\n## Check if a multi-level condition is currently met (all levels at required rank).\n## @param multi_condition: A dictionary from MULTI_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS.\n## @return: true if all levels meet their minimum rank requirement.\nfunc is_multi_condition_met(multi_condition: Dictionary) -> bool:\n\tfor level_entry in multi_condition.get(\"levels\", []):\n\t\tvar path: String = level_entry.get(\"path\", \"\")\n\t\tvar min_rank: String = level_entry.get(\"min_rank\", \"D\")\n\t\tvar best_rank: String = _get_best_rank_any_difficulty(path)\n\t\tif not _is_rank_sufficient(best_rank, min_rank):\n\t\t\treturn false\n\treturn true\n\n\n## Check if the all-difficulties unlock condition is currently met.\n## The condition is met when at least one level has been completed on every available difficulty.\n## @return: true if at least one level has been completed on each difficulty.\nfunc is_all_difficulties_condition_met() -> bool:\n\tvar progress_manager: Node = get_node_or_null(\"/root/ProgressManager\")\n\tif not progress_manager:\n\t\treturn false\n\tfor difficulty_name in _get_all_difficulty_names():\n\t\tvar found: bool = false\n\t\tfor key in progress_manager.get_all_progress():\n\t\t\t# Key format: \"level_path:difficulty_name\"\n\t\t\tif key.ends_with(\":\" + difficulty_name):\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\tif not found:\n\t\t\treturn false\n\treturn true\n\n\n## Check if a kill-based unlock condition is currently met.\n## @param kill_condition: A dictionary from KILL_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS.\n## @return: true if the qualifying kill count meets or exceeds min_kills.\nfunc is_kill_condition_met(kill_condition: Dictionary) -> bool:\n\tvar game_manager: Node = get_node_or_null(\"/root/GameManager\")\n\tif not game_manager:\n\t\treturn false\n\tvar stat: String = kill_condition.get(\"stat\", \"\")\n\tvar min_kills: int = kill_condition.get(\"min_kills\", 0)\n\tif stat.is_empty():\n\t\treturn false\n\tvar current_kills: int = game_manager.get(stat) if game_manager.get(stat) != null else 0\n\treturn current_kills >= min_kills\n\n\n## Get all weapon IDs that are unlockable via level conditions (have a condition defined).\n## @return: Array of weapon IDs that have unlock conditions.\nfunc get_weapons_with_conditions() -> Array[String]:\n\tvar result: Array[String] = []\n\tfor condition_key in UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tvar condition: Dictionary = UNLOCK_CONDITIONS[condition_key]\n\t\tfor multi_condition.get(\"weapons\", []):\n\t\t\tif weapon_id not in result:\n\t\t\t\tresult.append(weapon_id)\n\tfor kill_condition in KILL_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tfor weapon_id in kill_condition.get(\"weapons\", []):\n\t\t\tif weapon_id not in result:\n\t\t\t\tresult.append(weapon_id)\n\tfor all_diff_condition in ALL_DIFFICULTIES_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tfor weapon_id in KILL_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tfor grenade_type in kill_condition.get(\"grenades\", []):\n\t\t\tif grenade_type not in result:\n\t\t\t\tresult.append(grenade_type)\n\tfor all_diff_condition in is_grenade_condition_met(grenade_type: int) -> bool:\n\tfor condition_key in UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tvar condition: Dictionary = UNLOCK_CONDITIONS[condition_key]\n\t\tif grenade_type in all_diff_condition.get(\"grenades\", []):\n\t\t\tif is_all_difficulties_condition_met():\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\treturn false\n\n\n## Check if there is any item (weapon, grenade, or active item) whose unlock condition\n## is met but which has not yet been unlocked by the player.\n## Used to highlight the Armory button in the pause menu and score screen.\n## @return: true if at least one item is ready to unlock (condition met, but still locked).\nfunc has_any_available_unlock() -> bool:\n\tvar game_manager: Node = get_node_or_null(\"/root/GameManager\")\n\tvar active_item_manager: Node = get_node_or_null(\"/root/ActiveItemManager\")\n\tvar grenade_manager: Node = get_node_or_null(\"/root/GrenadeManager\")\n\n\tfor condition_key in UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tif not is_condition_key_met(condition_key):\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\tvar condition: Dictionary = UNLOCK_CONDITIONS[condition_key]\n\n\t\t# Check condition-gated weapons\n\t\tif game_manager:\n\t\t\tfor weapon_id in condition.get(\"weapons\", []):\n\t\t\t\tif game_manager.has_method(\"is_weapon_unlocked\") and not game_manager.is_weapon_unlocked(weapon_id):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t\t# Check condition-gated active items\n\t\tif active_item_manager:\n\t\t\tfor item_type in condition.get(\"active_items\", []):\n\t\t\t\tif active_item_manager.has_method(\"is_active_item_unlocked\") and not active_item_manager.is_active_item_unlocked(item_type):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t\t# Check condition-gated grenades\n\t\tif grenade_manager:\n\t\t\tfor grenade_type in condition.get(\"grenades\", []):\n\t\t\t\tif grenade_manager.has_method(\"is_grenade_unlocked\") and not grenade_manager.is_grenade_unlocked(grenade_type):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t# Check multi-level conditions\n\tfor multi_condition in MULTI_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tif not is_multi_condition_met(multi_condition):\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\n\t\tif game_manager:\n\t\t\tfor weapon_id in multi_condition.get(\"weapons\", []):\n\t\t\t\tif game_manager.has_method(\"is_weapon_unlocked\") and not game_manager.is_weapon_unlocked(weapon_id):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t\tif active_item_manager:\n\t\t\tfor item_type in multi_condition.get(\"active_items\", []):\n\t\t\t\tif active_item_manager.has_method(\"is_active_item_unlocked\") and not active_item_manager.is_active_item_unlocked(item_type):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t\tif grenade_manager:\n\t\t\tfor grenade_type in multi_condition.get(\"grenades\", []):\n\t\t\t\tif grenade_manager.has_method(\"is_grenade_unlocked\") and not grenade_manager.is_grenade_unlocked(grenade_type):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t# Check kill-based conditions\n\tfor kill_condition in KILL_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tif not is_kill_condition_met(kill_condition):\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\n\t\tif game_manager:\n\t\t\tfor weapon_id in kill_condition.get(\"weapons\", []):\n\t\t\t\tif game_manager.has_method(\"is_weapon_unlocked\") and not game_manager.is_weapon_unlocked(weapon_id):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t\tif active_item_manager:\n\t\t\tfor item_type in kill_condition.get(\"active_items\", []):\n\t\t\t\tif active_item_manager.has_method(\"is_active_item_unlocked\") and not active_item_manager.is_active_item_unlocked(item_type):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t\tif grenade_manager:\n\t\t\tfor grenade_type in kill_condition.get(\"grenades\", []):\n\t\t\t\tif grenade_manager.has_method(\"is_grenade_unlocked\") and not grenade_manager.is_grenade_unlocked(grenade_type):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t# Check all-difficulties conditions\n\tif is_all_difficulties_condition_met():\n\t\tfor all_diff_condition in ALL_DIFFICULTIES_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\t\tif game_manager:\n\t\t\t\tfor weapon_id in all_diff_condition.get(\"weapons\", []):\n\t\t\t\t\tif game_manager.has_method(\"is_weapon_unlocked\") and not game_manager.is_weapon_unlocked(weapon_id):\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t\t\tif active_item_manager:\n\t\t\t\tfor item_type in all_diff_condition.get(\"active_items\", []):\n\t\t\t\t\tif active_item_manager.has_method(\"is_active_item_unlocked\") and not active_item_manager.is_active_item_unlocked(item_type):\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t\t\tif grenade_manager:\n\t\t\t\tfor grenade_type in all_diff_condition.get(\"grenades\", []):\n\t\t\t\t\tif grenade_manager.has_method(\"is_grenade_unlocked\") and not grenade_manager.is_grenade_unlocked(grenade_type):\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\treturn false\n\n\n## Reset all condition-gated items to progress_manager:\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\n\tvar best_rank: String = \"\"\n\tfor difficulty_name in _get_all_difficulty_names():\n\t\tvar rank: String = progress_manager.get_best_rank(level_path, requirement.\n## @param rank: The rank to check.\n## @param min_rank: The minimum rank required.\n## @return: true if rank is >= min_rank.\nfunc _is_rank_sufficient(rank: String, min_rank: String) -> bool:\n\tif min_rank == \"F\":\n\t\t# F or higher means any completed run counts\n\t\treturn not rank.is_empty()\n\treturn not _is_rank_better(min_rank, rank)  # min_rank is not better than rank => rank >= min_rank\n\n\n## Compare two ranks. Returns true if new_rank is strictly better than old_rank.\nfunc _is_rank_better(new_rank: String, old_rank: String) -> bool:\n\tif old_rank.is_empty():\n\t\treturn true\n\tvar new_index: int = RANK_ORDER.find(new_rank)\n\tvar old_index: int = RANK_ORDER.find(old_rank)\n\tif new_index == -1:\n\t\treturn false\n\tif old_index == -1:\n\t\treturn true\n\treturn new_index > old_index\n\n\n## Extract the actual scene path from a condition key.\n## Keys may have a rank suffix like \"res://scenes/levels/BuildingLevel.tscn:S\".\n## Returns the scene path part only.\nfunc _extract_scene_path(condition_key: String) -> String:\n\t# Strip \":RANK\" suffix if present (the suffix won't start with \"res://\")\n\tvar last_colon: int = condition_key.rfind(\":\")\n\tif last_colon > 0 and not condition_key.substr(0, last_colon).ends_with(\"//\"):\n\t\t# The suffix is a rank like \":S\", \":D\", etc. (short, no slashes)\n\t\tvar suffix: String = condition_key.substr(last_colon + 1)\n\t\tif suffix in RANK_ORDER:\n\t\t\treturn condition_key.substr(0, last_colon)\n\treturn condition_key\n\n\n## Check if a specific condition key's unlock ready to unlock (condition met, but still locked).\nfunc has_any_available_unlock() -> bool:\n\tvar game_manager: Node = get_node_or_null(\"/root/GameManager\")\n\tvar active_item_manager: Node = []):\n\t\t\t\tif game_manager.has_method(\"is_weapon_unlocked\") and not game_manager.is_weapon_unlocked(weapon_id):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t\tif active_item_manager:\n\t\t\tfor item_type in grenade_manager.is_grenade_unlocked(grenade_type):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t# Check kill-based conditions\n\tfor kill_condition in KILL_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tif not kill_condition.get(\"grenades\", []):\n\t\t\t\tif grenade_manager.has_method(\"is_grenade_unlocked\") and not grenade_manager.is_grenade_unlocked(grenade_type):\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t# Check active_item_manager:\n\t\t\t\tfor item_type in all_diff_condition.get(\"active_items\", []):\n\t\t\t\t\tif active_item_manager.has_method(\"is_active_item_unlocked\") and not active_item_manager.is_active_item_unlocked(item_type):\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\t\t\tif grenade_manager:\n\t\t\t\tfor grenade_type in all_diff_condition.get(\"grenades\", []):\n\t\t\t\t\tif grenade_manager.has_method(\"is_grenade_unlocked\") and not grenade_manager.is_grenade_unlocked(grenade_type):\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n\n\treturn false\n\n\n## Reset all condition-gated items to unlock).\n## Returns a value in [0.0, 1.0] clamped to the condition's min_kills threshold.\n## Issue #1591: used by the armory to show progress bar animation.\nfunc get_weapon_kill_condition_progress(weapon_id: String) -> float:\n\tfor kill_condition in KILL_UNLOCK_CONDITIONS:\n\t\tif weapon_id in kill_condition.get(\"weapons\", []):\n\t\t\tvar game_manager: Node = get_node_or_null(\"/root/GameManager\")\n\t\t\tif not game_manager:\n\t\t\t\treturn 0.0\n\t\t\tvar stat: String = kill_condition.get(\"stat\", \"\")\n\t\t\tvar min_kills: int = kill_condition.get(\"min_kills\", 1)\n\t\t\tvar current: int = game_manager.get(stat) if game_manager.get(stat) != null else 0\n\t\t\treturn clampf(float(current) / float(min_kills), 0.0, 1.0)\n\treturn -1.0\n\n\n## Get the kill-based unlock condition progress for a grenade type (0.0–1.0).\n## Returns -1.0 if no kill-based condition applies.\n## Issue #1591: used by the armory to show canvas_layer)\\\\n00306| \\\\t\\\\tprint(\\\\\\\"[BuildingLevel] Weapon hints component added and setup\\\\\\\")\\\\n00307| \\\\n00308| \\\\n00309| ## Setup warm ceiling lights in the centers of large rooms (Issue #1206).\\\\n00310| ## Adds PointLight2D nodes with warm yellow-orange color to make the rooms\\\\n00311| ## look cozy and aesthetically pleasing.\\\\n00312| ##\\\\n00313| ## ## Light placement rules\\\\n00314| ## 1. Default position: geometric center of the room (average of its bounding box).\\\\n00315| ## 2. If a large obstacle (table, server rack, wall junction, etc.) sits at the\\\\n00316| ##    geometric center, shift the light to the nearest open area — typically\\\\n00317| ##    offset toward the side that has the most free floor space.\\\\n00318| ## 3. If a wall junction or shadow-casting surface is close (< 30 px) to the\\\\n00319| ##    light source, move the light inward until it is fully inside the open\\\\n00320| ##    floor area so shadows don't block the cone.\\\\n00321| ## 4. Prefer the upper half of a room when the lower half is crowded or when\\\\n00322| ##    the room label (\\\\\\\"OFFICE 2\\\\\\\", etc.) already anchors the top edge visually.\\\\n00323| ##\\\\n00324| ## Room centers (derived from RoomLabel bounds in the scene):\\\\n00325| ## - Conference Room: ~(1918, 340)  — geometric center, no obstacles\\\\n00326| ## - Break Room:      ~(1918, 994)  — geometric center, no obstacles\\\\n00327| ## - Server Room:     ~(2200, 1638) — shifted right, away from right-wall junction\\\\n00328| ## - Main Hall:       ~(1200, 1724) — geometric center, no obstacles\\\\n00329| ## - Office 1:        ~(290, 384)   — geometric center, no obstacles\\\\n00330| ## - Office 2:        ~(718, 780)   — shifted up from center (856) to upper half\\\\n00331| func _setup_room_warm_lights() -> void:\\\\n00332| \\\\tvar environment := get_node_or_null(\\\\\\\"Environment\\\\\\\")\\\\n00333| \\\\tif environment == null:\\\\n00334| \\\\t\\\\treturn\\\\n00335| \\\\n00336| \\\\t# Container node for all room lights\\\\n00337| \\\\tvar room_lights_node := Node2D.new()\\\\n00338| \\\\troom_lights_node.name = \\\\\\\"RoomLights\\\\\\\"\\\\n00339| \\\\tenvironment.add_child(room_lights_node)\\\\n00340| \\\\n00341| \\\\t# Large rooms get prominent warm lights; smaller rooms get subtler ones.\\\\n00342| \\\\t# Format: [position, energy, texture_scale, label]\\\\n00343| \\\\tvar room_configs: Array = [\\\\n00344| \\\\t\\\\t# Large rooms — bigger lights\\\\n00345| \\\\t\\\\t[Vector2(1918, 340),  0.9, 5.0, \\\\\\\"ConferenceRoom\\\\\\\"],\\\\n00346| \\\\t\\\\t[Vector2(1918, 994),  0.9, 5.0, \\\\\\\"BreakRoom\\\\\\\"],\\\\n00347| \\\\t\\\\t[Vector2(2200, 1638), 0.9, 5.0, \\\\\\\"ServerRoom\\\\\\\"],\\\\n00348| \\\\t\\\\t[Vector2(1200, 1724), 0.85, 4.5, \\\\\\\"MainHall\\\\\\\"],\\\\n00349| \\\\t\\\\t# Smaller rooms — softer lights\\\\n00350| \\\\t\\\\t[Vector2(290, 384),   0.7, 3.5, \\\\\\\"Office1\\\\\\\"],\\\\n00351| \\\\t\\\\t# Office 2: shifted to upper half (y=780 instead of centre y=856)\\\\n00352| \\\\t\\\\t# so the glow covers the label zone and the lower-half corridor approach.\\\\n00353| \\\\t\\\\t[Vector2(718, 780),   0.7, 3.5, \\\\\\\"Office2\\\\\\\"],\\\\n00354| \\\\t]\\\\n00355| \\\\n00356| \\\\tfor cfg in room_configs:\\\\n00357| \\\\t\\\\t_create_room_warm_light(room_lights_node, cfg[0], cfg[1], cfg[2], cfg[3])\\\\n00358| \\\\n00359| \\\\tprint(\\\\\\\"[BuildingLevel] Warm ceiling lights placed in all rooms (Issue #1206)\\\\\\\")\\\\n00360| \\\\n00361| \\\\n00362| ## Create a single warm ceiling light at the given room-center position.\\\\n00363| ## Uses a soft radial gradient that fades smoothly to black, producing a natural\\\\n00364| ## \\\\\\\"overhead lamp\\\\\\\" feel with no hard visible edge.\\\\n00365| ## @param parent: Container node.\\\\n00366| ## @param pos: World-space position (room center).\\\\n00367| ## @param energy: Light brightness (0–1 range, typical 0.7–0.9).\\\\n00368| ## @param scale: Texture scale controlling the light radius.\\\\n00369| ## @param room_name: Name suffix for the node (debug convenience).\\\\n00370| func _create_room_warm_light(parent: Node2D, pos: Vector2, energy: float, scale: float, room_name: String) -> void:\\\\n00371| \\\\tvar light_node := Node2D.new()\\\\n00372| \\\\tlight_node.name = \\\\\\\"WarmLight_%s\\\\\\\" % room_name\\\\n00373| \\\\tlight_node.position = pos\\\\n00374| \\\\tparent.add_child(light_node)\\\\n00375| \\\\n00376| \\\\t# Small visual indicator — a dim warm-colored circle representing the lamp fixture.\\\\n00377| \\\\t# Uses Sprite2D (not Control/ColorRect) so it does NOT intercept mouse events and\\\\n00378| \\\\t# cannot break pause-menu or UI clicks.\\\\n00379| \\\\tvar fixture := Sprite2D.new()\\\\n00380| \\\\tfixture.name = \\\\\\\"Fixture\\\\\\\"\\\\n00381| \\\\tfixture.texture = _create_lamp_fixture_texture()\\\\n00382| \\\\tfixture.modulate = Color(1.0, 0.85, 0.5, 0.5)  # Pale warm amber, semi-transparent\\\\n00383| \\\\tlight_node.add_child(fixture)\\\\n00384| \\\\n00385| \\\\t# The actual PointLight2D\\\\n00386| \\\\tvar light := PointLight2D.new()\\\\n00387| \\\\tlight.name = \\\\\\\"PointLight\\\\\\\"\\\\n00388| \\\\tlight.color = Color(1.0, 0.75, 0.3, 1.0)   # Warm amber-orange\\\\n00389| \\\\tlight.energy = energy\\\\n00390| \\\\tlight.shadow_enabled = true\\\\n00391| \\\\tlight.shadow_filter = PointLight2D.SHADOW_FILTER_PCF5\\\\n00392| \\\\tlight.shadow_filter_smooth = 4.0  # Soft shadow edges\\\\n00393| \\\\tlight.texture = _create_warm_light_texture()\\\\n00394| \\\\tlight.texture_scale = scale\\\\n00395| \\\\tlight_node.add_child(light)\\\\n00396| \\\\n00397| \\\\n00398| ## Create a soft radial gradient texture for the warm room lights.\\\\n00399| ## Uses a smooth natural falloff (bright core → gentle taper → complete black).\\\\n00400| ## No abrupt cutoff — the light fades organically like a real overhead lamp.\\\\n00401| func _create_warm_light_texture() -> ImageTexture:\\\\n00402| \\\\tvar size := 512\\\\n00403| \\\\tvar center := Vector2(size * 0.5, size * 0.5)\\\\n00404| \\\\tvar outer_r := size * 0.5  # 256 px\\\\n00405| \\\\n00406| \\\\tvar image := Image.create(size, size, false, Image.FORMAT_RGBA8)\\\\n00407| \\\\n00408| \\\\tfor y in range(size):\\\\n00409| \\\\t\\\\tfor x in range(size):\\\\n00410| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tvar dist := Vector2(x, y).distance_to(center)\\\\n00411| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tvar t := clampf(dist / outer_r, 0.0, 1.0)  # 0 = center, 1 = edge\\\\n00412| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t# Smooth inverse-square-ish falloff using a cosine curve:\\\\n00413| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t# bright centre → smooth mid-field → natural fade at rim\\\\n00414| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tvar brightness := pow(1.0 - t, 2.2)\\\\n00415| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\timage.set_pixel(x, y, Color(brightness, brightness, brightness, 1.0))\\\\n00416| \\\\n00417| \\\\treturn ImageTexture.create_from_image(image)\\\\n00418| \\\\n00419| \\\\n00420| ## Create a small circular texture for the lamp fixture visual indicator.\\\\n00421| ## Returns a soft-edged disc so the fixture looks like a round ceiling lamp,\\\\n00422| ## not a square. Drawn with per-pixel math so the disc has smooth anti-aliased edges.\\\\n00423| func _create_lamp_fixture_texture() -> ImageTexture:\\\\n00424| \\\\tvar size := 32\\\\n00425| \\\\tvar center := Vector2(size * 0.5, size * 0.5)\\\\n00426| \\\\tvar outer_r := size * 0.5  # full disc radius\\\\n00427| \\\\n00428| \\\\tvar image := Image.create(size, size, false, Image.FORMAT_RGBA8)\\\\n00429| \\\\n00430| \\\\tfor y in range(size):\\\\n00431| \\\\t\\\\tfor x in range(size):\\\\n00432| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tvar dist := Vector2(x, y).distance_to(center)\\\\n00433| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif dist >= outer_r:\\\\n00434| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\timage.set_pixel(x, y, Color(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0))\\\\n00435| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\telse:\\\\n00436| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t# Full brightness in the core, soft fade toward the rim\\\\n00437| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tvar t := clampf(dist / outer_r, 0.0, 1.0)\\\\n00438| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tvar alpha := pow(1.0 - t, 1.5)\\\\n00439| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\timage.set_pixel(x, y, Color(1.0, 1.0, 1.0, alpha))\\\\n00440| \\\\n00441| \\\\treturn ImageTexture.create_from_image(image)\\\\n00442| \\\\n00443| \\\\n00444| ## Setup window lights in corridors and rooms without enemies (Issue #593).\\\\n00445| ## Places dim blue PointLight2D nodes along exterior walls to simulate moonlight\\\\n00446| ## coming through windows. Only in areas without enemies so dark rooms with enemies\\\\n00447| ## remain tense and challenging.\\\\n00448| ## Window visuals (small blue ColorRect) are placed on the wall surface.\\\\n00449| ##\\\\n00450| ## Lighting architecture:\\\\n00451| ## - Each window has ONE primary PointLight2D (low energy, shadows on) for the\\\\n00452| ##   visible moonlight patch near the window.\\\\n00453| ## - ONE DirectionalLight2D provides scene-wide ambient moonlight glow with\\\\n00454| ##   NO visible edges. DirectionalLight2D illuminates the entire scene uniformly\\\\n00455| ##   (unlike PointLight2D which has a finite circular boundary).\\\\n00456| func _setup_window_lights() -> void:\\\\n00457| \\\\tvar environment := get_node_or_null(\\\\\\\"Environment\\\\\\\")\\\\n00458| \\\\tif environment == null:\\\\n00459| \\\\t\\\\treturn\\\\n00460| \\\\n00461| \\\\t# Create a container node for all window lights\\\\n00462| \\\\tvar windows_node := Node2D.new()\\\\n00463| \\\\twindows_node.name = \\\\\\\"WindowLights\\\\\\\"\\\\n00464| \\\\tenvironment.add_child(windows_node)\\\\n00465| \\\\n00466| \\\\t# Window light positions: [position, wall_side]\\\\n00467| \\\\t# wall_side: \\\\\\\"top\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"bottom\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"left\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"right\\\\\\\" determines window visual orientation\\\\n00468| \\\\t# Placed ONLY in corridors/rooms WITHOUT enemies:\\\\n00469| \\\\t# - Central corridor (x=512-1376, y=700-1012)\\\\n00470| \\\\t# - Left lobby area (x=64-900, y=1000-1400)\\\\n00471| \\\\t# - Storage room (x=80-500, y=1612-2048)\\\\n00472| \\\\n00473| \\\\t# Left wall windows (x=64) - lobby and storage areas (no enemies)\\\\n00474| \\\\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(64, 1100), \\\\\\\"left\\\\\\\")\\\\n00475| \\\\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(64, 1250), \\\\\\\"left\\\\\\\")\\\\n00476| \\\\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(64, 1750), \\\\\\\"left\\\\\\\")\\\\n00477| \\\\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(64, 1900), \\\\\\\"left\\\\\\\")\\\\n00478| \\\\n00479| \\\\t# Top wall windows (y=64) - above corridor area (no enemies in corridor)\\\\n00480| \\\\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(700, 64), \\\\\\\"top\\\\\\\")\\\\n00481| \\\\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(900, 64), \\\\\\\"top\\\\\\\")\\\\n00482| \\\\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(1100, 64), \\\\\\\"top\\\\\\\")\\\\n00483| \\\\n00484| \\\\t# Bottom wall windows (y=2064) - below storage and lobby (no enemies)\\\\n00485| \\\\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(200, 2064), \\\\\\\"bottom\\\\\\\")\\\\n00486| \\\\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(400, 2064), \\\\\\\"bottom\\\\\\\")\\\\n00487| \\\\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(700, 2064), \\\\\\\"bottom\\\\\\\")\\\\n00488| \\\\t_create_window_light(windows_node, Vector2(1100, 2064), \\\\\\\"bottom\\\\\\\")\\\\n00489| \\\\n00490| \\\\t# Scene-wide ambient moonlight using DirectionalLight2D.\\\\n00491| \\\\t# DirectionalLight2D illuminates the entire scene uniformly with NO visible\\\\n00492| \\\\t# edges (unlike PointLight2D which has a finite circular boundary).\\\\n00493| \\\\t# Energy is very low (0.06) so it provides visible wall outlines\\\\n00494| \\\\t# without washing out the scene or hiding weapon muzzle flashes.\\\\n00495| \\\\t_create_ambient_moonlight(windows_node)\\\\n00496| \\\\n00497| \\\\tprint(\\\\\\\"[BuildingLevel] Window lights placed in corridors without enemies (Issue #593)\\\\\\\")\\\\n00498| \\\\n00499| \\\\n00500| ## Create a single window light source at the given position on a wall.\\\\n00501| ## Creates ONE PointLight2D with shadows enabled — the visible moonlight patch\\\\n00502| ## near the window. Shadows from interior walls give the light a natural shape\\\\n00503| ## that respects the building's geometry.\\\\n00504| ## The scene-wide ambient glow is handled separately by _create_ambient_moonlight().\\\\n00505| ##\\\\n00506| ## Light gradient uses an early-fadeout design: the radial gradient reaches\\\\n00507| ## absolute zero at 55% of the radius, leaving 45% of the texture as pure black\\\\n00508| ## buffer. This ensures NO visible edges even against the near-black CanvasModulate.\\\\n00509| ## Combined with large texture_scale (6.0) and low energy (0.12), the light\\\\n00510| ## dissipates naturally with no perceptible boundary.\\\\n00511| ## @param parent: Parent node to add the window to.\\\\n00512| ## @param pos: Position of the window on the wall.\\\\n00513| ## @param wall_side: Which wall the window is on (\\\\\\\"top\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"bottom\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"left\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"right\\\\\\\").\\\\n00514| func _create_window_light(parent: Node2D, pos: Vector2, wall_side: String) -> void:\\\\n00515| \\\\tvar window_node := Node2D.new()\\\\n00516| \\\\twindow_node.name = \\\\\\\"Window_%s_%d_%d\\\\\\\" % [wall_side, int(pos.x), int(pos.y)]\\\\n00517| \\\\twindow_node.position = pos\\\\n00518| \\\\tparent.add_child(window_node)\\\\n00519| \\\\n00520| \\\\t# Create window visual (small blue rectangle on the wall)\\\\n00521| \\\\tvar window_rect := ColorRect.new()\\\\n00522| \\\\twindow_rect.color = Color(0.3, 0.4, 0.7, 0.6)  # Semi-transparent blue\\\\n00523| \\\\t# Size and offset depend on wall orientation\\\\n00524| \\\\tmatch wall_side:\\\\n00525| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"left\\\\\\\":\\\\n00526| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_left = -4.0\\\\n00527| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_top = -20.0\\\\n00528| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_right = 4.0\\\\n00529| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_bottom = 20.0\\\\n00530| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"right\\\\\\\":\\\\n00531| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_left = -4.0\\\\n00532| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_top = -20.0\\\\n00533| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_right = 4.0\\\\n00534| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_bottom = 20.0\\\\n00535| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"top\\\\\\\":\\\\n00536| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_left = -20.0\\\\n00537| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_top = -4.0\\\\n00538| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_right = 20.0\\\\n00539| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_bottom = 4.0\\\\n00540| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"bottom\\\\\\\":\\\\n00541| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_left = -20.0\\\\n00542| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_top = -4.0\\\\n00543| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_right = 20.0\\\\n00544| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\twindow_rect.offset_bottom = 4.0\\\\n00545| \\\\twindow_node.add_child(window_rect)\\\\n00546| \\\\n00547| \\\\t# --- Primary moonlight (visible patch near the window) ---\\\\n00548| \\\\tvar light := PointLight2D.new()\\\\n00549| \\\\tlight.name = \\\\\\\"MoonLight\\\\\\\"\\\\n00550| \\\\tlight.color = Color(0.4, 0.5, 0.9, 1.0)  # Cool blue moonlight\\\\n00551| \\\\tlight.energy = 0.12  # Low — slightly brighter (Issue #642), large texture_scale compensates for coverage\\\\n00552| \\\\t# Shadows enabled so interior walls cast natural shadows from the moonlight\\\\n00553| \\\\tlight.shadow_enabled = true\\\\n00554| \\\\tlight.shadow_filter = PointLight2D.SHADOW_FILTER_PCF5\\\\n00555| \\\\tlight.shadow_filter_smooth = 4.0  # Higher smoothing for softer shadow edges\\\\n00556| \\\\tlight.shadow_color = Color(0, 0, 0, 0.7)\\\\n00557| \\\\tlight.texture = _create_window_light_texture()\\\\n00558| \\\\tlight.texture_scale = 6.0  # Large scale so gradient fades out well before the edge\\\\n00559| \\\\t# Offset the light inward from the wall so it illuminates the interior\\\\n00560| \\\\tmatch wall_side:\\\\n00561| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"left\\\\\\\":\\\\n00562| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlight.position = Vector2(60, 0)\\\\n00563| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"right\\\\\\\":\\\\n00564| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlight.position = Vector2(-60, 0)\\\\n00565| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"top\\\\\\\":\\\\n00566| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlight.position = Vector2(0, 60)\\\\n00567| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"bottom\\\\\\\":\\\\n00568| \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlight.position = Vector2(0, -60)\\\\n00569| \\\\twindow_node.add_child(light)\\\\n00570| \\\\n00571| \\\\n00572| ## Create a radial gradient texture for the primary window moonlight.\\\\n00573| ## Uses an early-fadeout design where the gradient reaches absolute zero at 55%\\\\n00574| ## of the radius, leaving 45% of the texture as pure black buffer zone.\\\\n00575| ## This eliminates visible edges at the PointLight2D quad boundary because the\\\\n00576| ## light contribution is already zero well before the texture boundary.\\\\n00577| ## Against the near-black CanvasModulate (0.02, 0.02, 0.04), even subpixel\\\\n00578| ## differences at the boundary could be visible, so the large buffer is critical.\\\\n00579| func _create_window_light_texture() -> GradientTexture2D:\\\\n00580| \\\\tvar gradient := Gradient.new()\\\\n00581| \\\\t# Bright center core (0-10% radius)\\\\n00582| \\\\tgradient.set_color(0, Color(1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0))\\\\n00583| \\\\t# Smooth falloff begins early\\\\n00584| \\\\tgradient.add_point(0.1, Color(0.7, 0.7, 0.7, 1.0))\\\\n00585| \\\\tgradient.add_point(0.2, Color(0.45, 0.45, 0.45, 1.0))\\\\n00586| \\\\tgradient.add_point(0.3, Color(0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 1.0))\\\\n00587| \\\\tgradient.add_point(0.4, Color(0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 1.0))\\\\n00588| \\\\t# Fade to absolute zero by 55% — remaining 45% is pure black buffer\\\\n00589| \\\\tgradient.add_point(0.5, Color(0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 1.0))\\\\n00590| \\\\tgradient.add_point(0.55, Color(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0))\\\\n00591| \\\\tgradient.set_color(1, Color(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0))\\\\n00592| \\\\n00593| \\\\tvar texture := GradientTexture2D.new()\\\\n00594| \\\\ttexture.gradient = gradient\\\\n00595| \\\\ttexture.width = 512\\\\n00596| \\\\ttexture.height = 512\\\\n00597| \\\\ttexture.fill = GradientTexture2D.FILL_RADIAL\\\\n00598| \\\\ttexture.fill_from = Vector2(0.5, 0.5)\\\\n00599| \\\\ttexture.fill_to = Vector2(0.5, 0.0)\\\\n00600| \\\\treturn texture\\\\n00601| \\\\n00602| \\\\n00603| ## Create a scene-wide ambient moonlight using DirectionalLight2D.\\\\n00604| ## DirectionalLight2D illuminates the entire scene uniformly — unlike PointLight2D,\\\\n00605| ## it has no position, no radius, and NO visible edges. This is the correct node\\\\n00606| ## for simulating moonlight (parallel rays from a distant source).\\\\n00607| ## Shadows are disabled so the ambient glow passes through all walls uniformly.\\\\n00608| ## @param parent: Parent node to add the ambient light to.\\\\n00609| func _create_ambient_moonlight(parent: Node2D) -> void:\\\\n00610| \\\\tvar ambient := DirectionalLight2D.new()\\\\n00611| \\\\tambient.name = \\\\\\\"AmbientMoonlight\\\\\\\"\\\\n00612| \\\\tambient.color = Color(0.35, 0.45, 0.85, 1.0)  # Subtle blue moonlight tint\\\\n00613| \\\\tambient.energy = 0.06  # Faint — slightly brighter (Issue #642) for better wall outline visibility\\\\n00614| \\\\tambient.shadow_enabled = false  # Must pass through all walls for uniform glow\\\\n00615| \\\\tparent.add_child(ambient)\\\\n00616| \\\\n00617| \\\\n00618| func _process(_delta: float) -> void:\\\\n00619| \\\\t# Update enemy positions for aggressiveness tracking\\\\n00620| \\\\tvar score_manager: Node = get_node_or_null(\\\\\\\"/root/ScoreManager\\\\\\\")\\\\n00621| \\\\tif score_manager and score_manager.has_method(\\\\\\\"update_enemy_positions\\\\\\\"):\\\\n00622| \\\\t\\\\tscore_manager.update_enemy_positions(_enemies)\\\\n00623| \\\\t# Issue #959: Re-check level completion when a retaliating pacifist finishes retaliation.\\\\n00624| \\\\tif _current_enemy_count <= 0 and not _level_cleared and not _has_retaliating_pacifists():\\\\n00625| \\\\t\\\\tprint(\\\\\\\"All enemies eliminated or pacified! \\\\t\\\\treturn false\\\\n00431| \\\\tif old_index == -1:\\\\n00432| \\\\t\\\\treturn true\\\\n00433| \\\\treturn new_index > old_index\\\\n00434| \\\\n00435| \\\\n00436| ## Extract the actual scene to make the rooms\\n00311| ## look cozy and aesthetically pleasing.\\n00312| ##\\n00313| ## ## Light placement rules\\n00314| ## 1. Default position: geometric center of the room (average of its bounding box).\\n00315| ## 2. If a large obstacle (table, server rack, wall junction, etc.) sits at the\\n00316| ##    geometric center, shift the light to the nearest open area — typically\\n00317| ##    offset toward the side that has the most free floor space.\\n00318| ## 3. If a wall junction or shadow-casting surface is close (< 30 px) to the\\n00319| ##    light source, move the light inward until it is fully inside the open\\n00320| ##    floor area so shadows don't block the cone.\\n00321| ## 4. Prefer the upper half of a room when the lower half is crowded or when\\n00322| ##    the room label (\\\"OFFICE 2\\\", etc.) already anchors the top edge visually.\\n00323| ##\\n00324| ## Room centers (derived from RoomLabel bounds in the scene):\\n00325| ## - Main Hall:       ~(1200, 1724) — geometric center, no obstacles\\n00329| ## - Office 1:        ~(290, 384)   — geometric center, no obstacles\\n00330| ## - Office 2:        ~(718, 780)   — shifted up from center (856) to upper half\\n00331| func _setup_room_warm_lights() -> void:\\n00332| \\tvar environment := get_node_or_null(\\\"Environment\\\")\\n00333| \\tif environment == null:\\n00334| \\t\\treturn\\n00335| \\n00336| \\t# Container node for all room lights\\n00337| \\tvar room_lights_node := Node2D.new()\\n00338| \\troom_lights_node.name = \\\"RoomLights\\\"\\n00339| \\tenvironment.add_child(room_lights_node)\\n00340| \\n00341| \\t# Large rooms get prominent warm lights; smaller rooms get subtler ones.\\n00342| \\t# Format: [position, energy, texture_scale, label]\\n00343| \\tvar room_configs: Array = [\\n00344| \\t\\t# Large rooms — bigger lights\\n00345| \\t\\t[Vector2(1918, 340),  0.9, 5.0, \\\"ConferenceRoom\\\"],\\n00346| \\t\\t[Vector2(1918, 994),  0.9, 5.0, \\\"BreakRoom\\\"],\\n00347| \\t\\t[Vector2(2200, 1638), 0.9, 5.0, \\\"ServerRoom\\\"],\\n00348| \\t\\t[Vector2(1200, 1724), 0.85, 4.5, \\\"MainHall\\\"],\\n00349| \\t\\t# Smaller rooms — softer lights\\n00350| \\t\\t[Vector2(290, 384),   0.7, 3.5, \\\"Office1\\\"],\\n00351| \\t\\t# Office 2: shifted to upper half (y=780 instead of centre y=856)\\n00352| \\t\\t# so the glow covers the label zone and the lower-half corridor approach.\\n00353| \\t\\t[Vector2(718, 780),   0.7, 3.5, \\\"Office2\\\"],\\n00354| \\t]\\n00355| \\n00356| \\tfor cfg in room_configs:\\n00357| \\t\\t_create_room_warm_light(room_lights_node, cfg[0], cfg[1], cfg[2], cfg[3])\\n00358| \\n00359| \\tprint(\\\"[BuildingLevel] Warm ceiling lights placed in all rooms (Issue #1206)\\\")\\n00360| \\n00361| \\n00362| ## Create a single warm ceiling light at the given room-center position.\\n00363| ## Uses a soft radial gradient that fades smoothly to black, producing a natural\\n00364| ## \\\"overhead lamp\\\" feel with no hard visible edge.\\n00365| ## @param parent: Container node.\\n00366| ## @param pos: World-space position (room center).\\n00367| ## @param energy: Light brightness (0–1 range, typical 0.7–0.9).\\n00368| ## @param scale: Texture scale controlling the light radius.\\n00369| ## @param room_name: Name suffix for the node (debug convenience).\\n00370| func _create_room_warm_light(parent: Node2D, pos: Vector2, energy: float, scale: float, room_name: String) -> void:\\n00371| \\tvar light_node := Node2D.new()\\n00372| \\tlight_node.name = \\\"WarmLight_%s\\\" % room_name\\n00373| \\tlight_node.position = pos\\n00374| \\tparent.add_child(light_node)\\n00375| \\n00376| \\t# Small visual indicator — a dim warm-colored circle representing the lamp fixture.\\n00377| \\t# Uses Sprite2D (not Control/ColorRect) so it does NOT intercept mouse events and\\n00378| \\t# cannot break pause-menu or UI clicks.\\n00379| \\tvar fixture := Sprite2D.new()\\n00380| \\tfixture.name = \\\"Fixture\\\"\\n00381| \\tfixture.texture = _create_lamp_fixture_texture()\\n00382| \\tfixture.modulate = Color(1.0, 0.85, 0.5, 0.5)  # Pale warm amber, semi-transparent\\n00383| \\tlight_node.add_child(fixture)\\n00384| \\n00385| \\t# The actual PointLight2D\\n00386| \\tvar light := PointLight2D.new()\\n00387| \\tlight.name = \\\"PointLight\\\"\\n00388| \\tlight.color = Color(1.0, 0.75, 0.3, 1.0)   # Warm amber-orange\\n00389| \\tlight.energy = energy\\n00390| \\tlight.shadow_enabled = true\\n00391| \\tlight.shadow_filter = PointLight2D.SHADOW_FILTER_PCF5\\n00392| \\tlight.shadow_filter_smooth = 4.0  # Soft shadow edges\\n00393| \\tlight.texture = _create_warm_light_texture()\\n00394| \\tlight.texture_scale = scale\\n00395| \\tlight_node.add_child(light)\\n00396| \\n00397| \\n00398| ## Create a soft radial gradient texture for the warm room lights.\\n00399| ## Uses a smooth natural falloff (bright core → gentle taper → complete black).\\n00400| ## No abrupt cutoff — the light fades organically like a real overhead lamp.\\n00401| func _create_warm_light_texture() -> ImageTexture:\\n00402| \\tvar size := 512\\n00403| \\tvar center := Vector2(size * 0.5, size * 0.5)\\n00404| \\tvar outer_r := size * 0.5  # 256 px\\n00405| \\n00406| \\tvar image := Image.create(size, size, false, Image.FORMAT_RGBA8)\\n00407| \\n00408| \\tfor y in range(size):\\n00409| \\t\\tfor x in range(size):\\n00410| \\t\\t\\tvar dist := Vector2(x, y).distance_to(center)\\n00411| \\t\\t\\tvar t := clampf(dist / outer_r, 0.0, 1.0)  # 0 = center, 1 = edge\\n00412| \\t\\t\\t# Smooth inverse-square-ish falloff using a cosine curve:\\n00413| \\t\\t\\t# bright centre → smooth mid-field → natural fade at rim\\n00414| \\t\\t\\tvar brightness := pow(1.0 - t, 2.2)\\n00415| \\t\\t\\timage.set_pixel(x, y, Color(brightness, brightness, brightness, 1.0))\\n00416| \\n00417| \\treturn ImageTexture.create_from_image(image)\\n00418| \\n00419| \\n00420| ## Create a small circular texture for the lamp fixture visual indicator.\\n00421| ## Returns a soft-edged disc so the fixture looks like a round ceiling lamp,\\n00422| ## not a square. Drawn with per-pixel math so the disc has smooth anti-aliased edges.\\n00423| func _create_lamp_fixture_texture() -> ImageTexture:\\n00424| \\tvar size := 32\\n00425| \\tvar center := Vector2(size * 0.5, size * 0.5)\\n00426| \\tvar outer_r := size * 0.5  # full disc radius\\n00427| \\n00428| \\tvar image := Image.create(size, size, false, Image.FORMAT_RGBA8)\\n00429| \\n00430| \\tfor y in range(size):\\n00431| \\t\\tfor x in range(size):\\n00432| \\t\\t\\tvar dist := Vector2(x, y).distance_to(center)\\n00433| \\t\\t\\tif dist >= outer_r:\\n00434| \\t\\t\\t\\timage.set_pixel(x, y, Color(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0))\\n00435| \\t\\t\\telse:\\n00436| \\t\\t\\t\\t# Full brightness in the core, soft fade toward the rim\\n00437| \\t\\t\\t\\tvar t := clampf(dist / outer_r, 0.0, 1.0)\\n00438| \\t\\t\\t\\tvar alpha := pow(1.0 - t, 1.5)\\n00439| \\t\\t\\t\\timage.set_pixel(x, y, Color(1.0, 1.0, 1.0, alpha))\\n00440| \\n00441| \\treturn ImageTexture.create_from_image(image)\\n00442| \\n00443| \\n00444| ## Setup window lights in corridors and rooms without enemies (Issue #593).\\n00445| ## Places dim blue PointLight2D nodes along exterior walls to simulate moonlight\\n00446| ## coming through windows. Only in areas without enemies so dark rooms with enemies\\n00447| ## remain tense and challenging.\\n00448| ## Window visuals (small blue ColorRect) are placed on the wall surface.\\n00449| ##\\n00450| ## Lighting architecture:\\n00451| ## - Each window has ONE primary PointLight2D (low energy, shadows on) for the\\n00452| ##   visible moonlight patch near the window.\\n00453| ## - ONE DirectionalLight2D provides scene-wide ambient moonlight glow with\\n00454| ##   NO visible edges. Shadows from interior walls give the light a natural shape\\n00503| ## that respects the building's geometry.\\n00504| ## The scene-wide ambient glow is handled separately by _create_ambient_moonlight().\\n00505| ##\\n00506| ## Light gradient uses an early-fadeout design: the radial gradient reaches\\n00507| ## absolute zero at 55% of the radius, leaving 45% of the texture as pure black\\n00508| ## buffer. This ensures NO visible edges even against the near-black CanvasModulate.\\n00509| ## Combined with large texture_scale (6.0) and low energy (0.12), the light\\n00510| ## dissipates naturally with no (\\\"top\\\", \\\"bottom\\\", \\\"left\\\", \\\"right\\\").\\n00514| func _create_window_light(parent: Node2D, pos: Vector2, wall_side: String) -> void:\\n00515| \\tvar window_node := \\n00520| \\t# Create window visual (small blue rectangle on the wall)\\n00521| \\tvar window_rect := ColorRect.new()\\n00522| \\twindow_rect.color = Color(0.3, 0.4, 0.7, 0.6)  # Semi-transparent before the edge\\n00559| \\t# Offset the light inward from the wall so it illuminates the interior\\n00560| \\tmatch wall_side:\\n00561| \\t\\t\\\"left\\\":\\n00562| \\t\\t\\tlight.position = 0.25, 1.0))\\n00587| \\tgradient.add_point(0.4, Color(0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 1.0))\\n00588| \\t# Fade to absolute zero by 55% — remaining 45% is pure black buffer\\n00589| \\tgradient.add_point(0.5, Color(0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 1.0))\\n00590| \\tgradient.add_point(0.55, Color(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0))\\n00591| \\tgradient.set_color(1, Color(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0))\\n00592| \\n00593| \\tvar texture := \\ttexture.fill_from = Vector2(0.5, 0.5)\\n00599| \\ttexture.fill_to = Vector2(0.5, 0.0)\\n00600| \\treturn texture\\n00601| \\n00602| \\n00603| ## Create a scene-wide ambient moonlight using This is the correct node\\n00606| ## for simulating moonlight (parallel rays from a distant source).\\n00607| ## Shadows are disabled so the ambient glow passes through all walls uniformly.\\n00608| ## @param parent: Parent node to add the ambient light to.\\n00609| func _create_ambient_moonlight(parent: Node2D) -> void:\\n00610| \\tvar ambient := DirectionalLight2D.new()\\n00611| \\tambient.name = \\\"AmbientMoonlight\\\"\\n00612| \\tambient.color = Color(0.35, 0.45, 0.85, 1.0)  # Subtle blue moonlight tint\\n00613| \\tambient.energy = 0.06  # Faint — slightly brighter (Issue #642) for better wall outline visibility\\n00614| \\tambient.shadow_enabled = false  # Must pass through all walls for uniform glow\\n00615| \\tparent.add_child(ambient)\\n00616| \\n00617| \\n00618| func _process(_delta: float) -> void:\\n00619| \\t# Update enemy positions for aggressiveness tracking\\n00620| \\tvar score_manager: Node = get_node_or_null(\\\"/root/ScoreManager\\\")\\n00621| \\tif score_manager and score_manager.has_method(\\\"update_enemy_positions\\\"):\\n00622| \\t\\tscore_manager.update_enemy_positions(_enemies)\\n00623| \\t# Issue #959: Re-check level completion when a retaliating pacifist finishes retaliation.\\n00624| \\tif _current_enemy_count <= 0 and not _level_cleared and not _has_retaliating_pacifists():\\n00625| \\t\\tprint(\\\"All enemies eliminated or pacified! \\n00709| \\n00710| ## Setup the navigation mesh for enemy pathfinding.\\n00711| ## Bakes the NavigationPolygon using physics collision layer 4 (walls).\\n00712| func _setup_navigation() -> old_index == -1:\\n00432| \\t\\treturn true\\n00433| \\treturn new_index > old_index\\n00434| \\n00435| \\n00436| ## Extract the actual scene path from a condition key.\\n00437| ## Keys may have a
